title: The Air Force Act, 1950
full_text: The Air Force Act, 1950 वायु सेना अधिनियम, 1950 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 195045 Act Number: 45 Enactment Date: 1950-05-18 Act Year: 1950 Short Title: The Air Force Act, 1950 Hindi Title: वायु सेना अधिनियम, 1950 Long Title: An Act to consolidate and amend the law relating to the government of the Air Force. Ministry: Ministry of Defence Department: Department of Military Affairs Enforcement Date: 22-07-1950 Notification: 22nd July, 1950, vide Notification No. S.R.O. 124, dated 22nd July, see Gazette of India, 1950, Part II, sec. 4. TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles Show all section The Air Force Act, 1950 CHAPTER I PRELIMINARY CHAPER II SPECIAL PROVISIONS FOR THE APPLICATION OF ACT IN CERTAIN CASES CHAPTER III COMMISSION, APPOINTMENT AND ENROLMENT CHAPTER IV CONDITIONS OF SERVICE CHAPTER V SERVICE PRIVILEGES CHAPTER VI OFFENCES CHAPTER VII PUNISHMENTS CHAPTER VIII PENAL DEDUCTIONS CHPATER IX ARREST AND PROCEEDINGS BEFORE TRIAL CHAPTER X COURTS-MARTIAL CHAPTER XI PROCEDURE OF COURTS-MATRIAL CHAPTER XII CONFIRMATION AND REVISION CHAPTER XIII EXECUTION OF SENTENCES CHAPTER XIV PARDONS REMISSIONS AND SUSPENSIONS CHAPTER XV RULES CHAPTER XVI TRANSITORY PROVISIONS Section 1. Short title and commencement. Section 2. Persons subject to this Act. Section 3. Termination of application of the Act. Section 4. Definitions. Section 5. Application of Act to certain forces under the Central Government. Section 6. Special provision as to rank in certain cases. Section 7. Commanding Officer of person subject to Air Force law under clause (d) of section 2. Section 8. Officers exercising powers in certain cases. Section 9. Power to declare persons to be on active service. Section 10. Commission and appointment. Section 11. Ineligibility of aliens for enrolment. Section 12. Ineligibility of females for enrolment or employment. Section 13. Procedure before enrolling officer. Section 14. Mode of enrolment. Section 15. Validity of enrolment. Section 16. Persons to be attested. Section 17. Mode of attestation. Section 18. Tenure of service under the Act. Section 19. Termination of service by Central Government. Section 20. Retirement, release or discharge. Section 23. Certificate on termination of service. Section 24. Discharge or dismissal when out of India. Section 25. Authorised deductions only to be made from pay. Section 26. Remedy of aggrieved airmen. Section 27. Remedy of aggrieved officers. Section 28. Immunity from attachment. Section 29. Immunity from arrest for debt. Section 30. Immunity of persons attending courts-martial from arrest. Section 31. Privileges of reservists. Section 32. Priority in respect of Air Force personnel's litigation. Section 33. Saving of rights and privileges under other laws. Section 34. Offences in relation to the enemy and punishable with death. Section 35. Offences in relation to the enemy and not punishable with death. Section 36. Offences punishable more severely on active service than at other times. Section 37. Mutiny. Section 38. Desertion and aiding desertion. Section 39. Absence without leave. Section 40. Striking or threatening superior officer. Section 41. Disobedience to superior officer. Section 42. Insubordination and obstruction. Section 43. Fraudulent enrolment. Section 44. False answers on enrolment. Section 45. Unbecoming conduct Section 46. Certain forms of disgraceful conduct. Section 47. III-treating a subordinate Section 48. Intoxication. Section 49. Extortion and corruption. Section 54. Making away with equipment Section 55. Injury to property. Section 56. False accusation. Section 57. Falsifying official documents and false declaration. Section 58. Signing in blank and failure to report. Section 59. Offences relating to courts-martial. Section 60. False evidence Section 61. Unlawful detention of pay. Section 62. Offences in relation to aircraft and flying Section 63. Other offences relating to aircraft and flying. Section 64. Disobedience of lawful command of captain of aircraft. Section 65. Violation of good order and air force discipline. Section 66. Miscellaneous offences. Section 67. Attempt. Section 68. Abetment of offences that have been committed. Section 69. Abetment of offences punishable with death and not committed. Section 70. Abetment of offences punishable with imprisonment and not committed. Section 71. Civil offences Section 72. Combination of punishments. Section 76. Cashiering of officers Section 77. Field punishment. Section 78. Position of field punishment in scale of punishments Section 79. Result of certain punishments in the case of a warrant officer or a non-commissioned officer Section 80. Retention in the ranks of a person convicted on active service. Section 81. Punishments otherwise than by court-martial Section 82. Punishment of persons other than officers and warrant officers. Section 83. Requirement of sanction in certain cases Section 84. Limit of punishments under section 82. Section 85. Punishments in addition to those specified in section 82 Section 86. Punishment of officers and warrant officers. Section 87. Transmission of proceedings. Section 88. Review of proceedings Section 89. Superior air force authority Section 90. Collective fines. Section 91. Deductions from pay and allowances of officers. Section 92. Deductions from pay and allowances of airmen Section 93. Computation of time of absence of custody. Section 94. Pay and allowances daring trial. Section 95. Limit of certain deductions. Section 96. Deduction from public money due to a person. Section 97. Pay and allowances of prisoner of war during inquiry into his conduct. Section 98. Remission of deductions. Section 99. Provision for dependants of prisoner of war from remitted deductions. Section 100. Provision for dependants of prisoner of war from his pay and allowances. Section 101. Period during which a person is deemed to be a prisoner of war Section 102. Custody of offenders Section 103. Duty of commanding officer in regard to detention. Section 104. Section 122. Liability of offender who ceases to be subject to Act. Section 123. Place of trial Section 124. Choice between criminal court and court-martial. Section 125. Power of criminal court to require delivery of offender. Section 126. Successive trials by a criminal, court and a court-martial. Section 127. Presiding Officer. Section 128. Judge Advocate. Section 129. Challenges Section 130. Oaths of member, Judge advocate and witness. Section 131. Voting by members Section 132. General rule as to evidence. Section 133. Judicial notice. Section 134. Summoning witnesses Section 135. Documents exempted from production. Section 136. Commissions for examination of witnesses. Section 137. Examination of a witness on commission. Section 138. Reference by accused to Government officer. Section 143. Evidence of previous convictions and general character Section 144. Lunacy of accused Section 145. Subsequent fitness of lunatic accused for trial. Section 146. Transmission to Central Government of orders under section 145. Section 147. Release of lunatic accused. Section 148. Delivery of lunatic accused to relatives Section 149. Order for custody and disposal of property pending trial Section 150. Order for disposal of property regarding which offence is committed Section 151. Powers of courts-martial when certain offences are committed by persons not subject to this Act. Section 152. Finding and sentence not valid, unless confirmed. Section 153. Power to confirm finding and sentence of general court-martial. Section 154. Power to confirm finding and sentence of summary general courts- martial. Section 157. Power of confirming authority to mitigate, remit or commute sentences. Section 158. Release on suspension. Section 183. Computation of period of suspension. Section 184. Order after suspension. Section 185. Reconsideration of case after suspension Section 186. Fresh sentence after suspension. Section 187. Scope of power of suspension Section 188. Effect of suspension and remission on dismissal. Section 189. Power to make rules. Show Related Subordinates Section 190. Power to make regulations Section 191. Publication of rules and regulations in Gazette. Section 191A. Laying of rules and regulations before Parliament. Section 192. Repealed. Section 193. Definition of British officer. Section 194. Powers of British officer.
